vo2Max.name: VO2 Max Calculator
vo2Max.description: Calculate your maximum oxygen uptake and fitness category.

meta.title: VO2 Max Calculator — Calculate Maximum Oxygen Uptake
meta.description: Calculate your VO2 Max with the Cooper Test, Rockport Walk Test or direct entry. Fitness category, age comparison and percentiles. Free, instant, no sign-up.

vo2Max.title: VO2 Max Calculator
vo2Max.description: Calculate your maximum oxygen uptake and find out how fit you really are.
vo2Max.cooperInfo: Run for 12 minutes as far as you can and enter the distance in meters.
vo2Max.rockportInfo: Walk 1 mile (1,609 m) as fast as you can and measure your heart rate at the end.
vo2Max.directInfo: Enter your known VO2 Max value directly.

faq.a: VO2max is the maximum amount of oxygen the body can utilize per minute (mL/kg/min). It is regarded as the gold-standard marker of cardiorespiratory fitness. Source: American Heart Association.
Men 30–39: average 31–40 mL/kg/min. Women 30–39: average 27–35. Elite endurance athletes reach 70–90 mL/kg/min. Source: ACSM.
Using the 1-mile Rockport walking test, which estimates VO2max from weight, time, end-of-test heart rate, and sex. Accuracy: ±10–15 % vs. lab spirometry.

faq.q: How can I improve my VO2max?
faq.a: High-intensity interval training (HIIT) combined with long aerobic base work is most effective. Expect +10–25 % in 8–12 weeks if untrained.
Yes — roughly 1 % per year from age 30 onward. Regular endurance training can halve or reverse this decline.

faq.q: How is VO2max linked to lifespan?
faq.a: Low VO2max is a stronger predictor of early mortality than smoking or diabetes (JAMA 2018). Improving VO2max is associated with lower mortality.
